calendar.matters:
Trustee Bank Of America N.A.'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Sections 128.7 And 1008(D); Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Nov-15-2012 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Eugene Hom for Bank of America (Trustee). Sam Ware in Propria Persona. Sam Sloan in Propria Persona.

Mr. Hom and Mr. Sloan presented oral argument to support their positions. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court denied the Motion for Sanctions. Mr. Ware is to prepare the order.

Judge: Peter Busch. Court Reporter: Adrienne Meda, CSR#6609. Phone Number: 415-472-2361. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel
Off Calendar Sep-20-2012 Advanced From Oct-30-12 To Oct-29-12 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204

Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel
Off Calendar Nov-15-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Sam Ware for and with Sam Sloan. Arden Van Upp (Friend of Sam Sloan) also present.

The Court reminds the Parties of the request to have the matter heard in camera. The Courtroom was locked and no other Parties other than Court staff, the Reporter and Ms. Van Upp were present. Mr. Ware stated that he was okay with Ms. Van Upp remaining in the courtroom. Mr. Sloan also stated that he wanted Ms. Van Upp to remain in the courtroom, that he was waiving all priveledges and that he is waiving having the transcripts sealed. The Parties presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to Mr. Ware withdrawing as Counsel. The Court granted the Motion to Be Relieved as Counsel (subject to Mr. Ware preparing the order on the mandatory Judicial Counsel form).

Judge: Peter Busch. Court Reporter: Adrienne Meda, CSR#6609. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy F. Ruiz.

Trustee Bank Of America N.A.'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Sections 128.7 And 1008(D); Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Sep-20-2012 Continued To Nov-15-12 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Eugene Hom for Bank of America (Trustee). Sam Ware for and with Sam Sloan.

Mr. Ware stated that he can not explain circumstances/provide information unless he is allowed to do speak to the Court in camera, that he would like the opportunity to file further opposition to the Motion for Sanctions but after the Motion to Be Relieved As Counsel is heard because he will then be in a different position. Mr. Hom objected to the continuance, stated that he sees no basis for it, raised the issue of residency of the property and stated that there was an unlawful detainer action pending. The Court granted the continuance. The Motion for Sanctions was continued to November 15, 2012 at 2:00 p.m., in Dept. 204. The Court asked Mr. Ware to have his supplemental opposition ready to go by November 1, 2012 as well as Mr. Sloan if he ends up representing himself. The supplemental reply by Mr. Hom is due by November 7, 2012. Mr. Hom reserved the right to respond to any supplement filed by Mr. Sloan. The Parties agreed to have the Motion to Be Relieved As Counsel advanced from October 30, 2012 to October 29, 2012 at 10:30 a.m.

Judge: Peter Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Santiago.

Trustee Bank Of America N.A.'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Sections 128.7 And 1008(D); Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Aug-14-2012 Continued To Sep-20-12 At 02:30 P.M. In Dept 204

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Production Of Documents And Further Deposition And Declaration Of Sloan In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Jul-19-2012 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Sam Ware for and with Sam Sloan (Administrator). Gary Rothstein for Bank of America. Alicia Adornato for Guide Dogs for the Blind.

Counsel presented oral argument to support their respective positions. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling, the Motion to Reconsider is denied. The Court is not imposing sanctions. Mr. Rothstein was instructed to prepare the order denying the motion.

Judge: Peter Busch. Court Reporter: Noel Degnan CSR #6921. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy Chien.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Production Of Documents And Further Deposition And Declaration Of Sloan In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Jun-05-2012 Continued To Jul-19-12 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Sam Slaon For Forensic Documets Examination Of Documents Purported To Be Trust Documents Of The Goodall Family Trust.
Off Calendar May-07-2012 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Gary Rothstein for Bank of America (Trustee). Alicia Adornato for Guide Dog For The Blind. Sam Ware for and with Sam Sloan.

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court denied Sam Sloan's Petition for Forensic Document Examination and granted the Bank's Petition for Determination of The Trust Beneficiaries to Whom Trust Property Shall Pass Upon Termination of Trust determining that Guide Dogs for The Blind is the residual Beneficiary. Mr. Rothsteins is to prepare the orders.

Judge: Peter Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Clauzel.

Petition For Determination Of The Beneficiaries To Whom Trust Property Shall Pass Upon Termination Of Trust
Off Calendar May-15-2012 Text Ruling

Petition For Determination Of The Beneficiaries To Whom Trust Property Shall Pass Upon Termination Of Trust
Off Calendar Mar-07-2012 Continued To May-07-12 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Gary Rothstein for Bank of America. Alicia Adornato for Guide Dogs For The Blind. Sam Ware for and with Sam Sloan.

The Petition for Determination of The Beneficiaries to Whom Trust Property Shall Pass Upon Termination of Trust was continued to May 7, 2012 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Ware will amend the petition that was filed by his Client on March 1, 2012. The Court ordered that Mr. Sloan is to pay rent commencing March 1, 2012 and continue to pay rent until the matter is resolved. Counsel are to work on determining what fair market rental value is and the amount of rent to be paid. Mr. Rothstein will prepare the order.

Judge: John Dearman. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Gomez.

Petition For Determination Of The Beneficiaries To Whom Trust Property Shall Pass Upon Termination Of Trust

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Production Of Documents And Further Deposition And Declaration Of Sam Ware In Support Thereof And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Jan-12-2012 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Sam Ware for and with Sam Sloan. Gary Rothstein for Bank of America (Trustee). Don Lesser for James Hastings.

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions. The Motion to Compel Production of Documents, etc. is denied subject to Mr. Hastings providing a further declaration within 10-days expressing that either he has made a further good faith effort to locate the file but has not found it or if he finds the file, that it is produced. Mr. Lesser is to prepare the order.

Judge: Peter Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera.

Off Calendar Nov-15-2011 Continued To Dec-21-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Determination Of The Beneficiaries To Whom Trust Property Shall Pass Upon Termination Of Trust
Off Calendar Aug-23-2011 Continued To Nov-15-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Gary Rothstein for Bank of America (Trustee). John McDonnell, Jr. for Guide Dogs. Sam Ware for and with Sam Sloan. James Hastings drafter of the Goodall Trust also present.

Mr. Ware stated that he has an executed Substitution of Attorney, and requested more time to look into the matter and have the opportunity to conduct discovery. The matter was continued to November 15, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of a hearing on the Petition to Determine Beneficiaries.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy F. Smith.

Petition For Determination Of The Beneficiaries To Whom Trust Property Shall Pass Upon Termination Of Trust
Off Calendar Jul-11-2011 Continued To Aug-23-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Sam Sloan Aministrator of the Estate of K. Micheal Goodall in Marin County.

This was a continued matter. Mr. Sloan argued that the Trust should be in Marin County and stated that he was not given notice of the petition. The Court advised Mr. Sloan to contact Counsel for the moving Party to ask for notice of the continued hearing and the petition, and to check the tentative ruling line for future hearings. The matter remains continued to August 23, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Petition For Order Confirming Real Rpoperty As An Asset Of The Trust
Off Calendar Jun-03-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Sheldon Wolfe for Bank of America (Trustee). Sam Sloan (Objector) in Propria Persona. Roy Hoppe also present.

The Parties presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to venue/jurisdiction and whether the Peach Stone property is/is not an asset of the Trust. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Chien.
